message: Fix newproj AGENTS template backup deletion

@@ -632,6 +632,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-f35df","title":"pcr: Create onboarding lesson","description":"Create onboarding lesson for post_compact_reminder.

Cover:
1. Why context compaction is dangerous:
   - Claude forgets AGENTS.md rules, project conventions, recent decisions
   - Can lead to destructive actions (wrong branch, deleted files, skipped hooks)
   - Compaction happens automatically when context window fills up

2. How PCR works:
   - Hooks into Claude Code's SessionStart event with matcher: 'compact'
   - After compaction, PCR fires and reminds Claude to re-read AGENTS.md
   - Four templates: minimal, detailed, checklist, default
   - All templates require Claude to confirm before proceeding

3. Status check: install-post-compact-reminder.sh --status

4. Customization:
   - Templates can be customized at ~/.config/pcr/template.md (if supported)
   - Or modify the hook script directly

5. What happens if PCR is not installed:
   - Claude may forget critical project rules after compaction
   - You'd need to manually remind Claude in every post-compaction message

Keep practical, 3 min read. This is a simple "install and forget" tool.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-03-12T19:43:50.097348231Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T19:43:50.096241479Z","close_reason":"Completed: onboarding lesson added and corrected in acfs/onboard/lessons/38_pcr.md with install, verification, and customization guidance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f35df","depends_on_id":"bd-52f4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T13:00:00Z","created_by":"claude","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-f5m0","title":"Update manifest generator to include Claude fallback pattern and security fixes","description":"The install_agents.sh file is generated from acfs.manifest.yaml but has been manually customized with: (1) Fallback pattern that tries verified install first, then falls back to direct curl install, (2) Robust associative array check using grep for 'declare -A', (3) Safe variable initialization before array access. These customizations will be LOST if someone runs 'bun run generate'. The generator in packages/manifest/src/generate.ts should be updated to include this pattern so regeneration preserves these improvements.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T07:40:18.360744Z","updated_at":"2025-12-22T07:47:32.095844Z","closed_at":"2025-12-22T07:47:32.095844Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Updated generator with resilient fallback pattern, added fallback_url field to schema, regenerated install_agents.sh","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-fac","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Infrastructure","description":"# Epic: Project Foundation & Infrastructure

## Overview
Set up the foundational infrastructure for the ACFS project, including repo structure, tooling, and development environment.

## Background & Reasoning
Before any feature development can begin, we need a solid foundation:
- Proper monorepo structure for website + installer + onboard
- Consistent tooling (bun, TypeScript, linting)
- CI/CD pipeline for quality gates
- Documentation standards

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All directory structure matches AGENTS.md spec
- [ ] bun workspace configured properly
- [ ] CI runs on every PR
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all bash scripts

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation

## Considerations
- Keep structure minimal initially, add complexity only when needed
- Prefer convention over configuration
- Make it easy for agents to understand and navigate

## Relationship to Project Goals
This enables all subsequent development by providing a consistent, well-documented codebase structure that both humans and AI agents can work with effectively.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:19:59.763401Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T04:24:12.701537Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T04:24:12.701537Z","close_reason":"Foundation complete: directory structure, install.sh, library scripts, configs all created","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","infrastructure"]}

 {"id":"bd-fed","title":"EPIC: Module Manifest System","description":"# Epic: Module Manifest System

## Overview
Create the module manifest system (acfs.manifest.yaml) that serves as single source of truth for all installed tools.

## Background & Reasoning
Without a manifest, we'd have:
- Scattered install logic
- No way to generate doctor checks automatically
- Website and installer out of sync
- Hard to add/update tools

The manifest provides:
- Canonical list of all tools
- Install commands
- Verify commands
- Dependencies between modules
- Metadata for website display

## Manifest Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe

modules:
  - id: stack.ntm
    description: Named tmux manager (agent cockpit)
    category: stack
    priority: 1
    install:
      - curl -fsSL https://... | bash
    verify:
      - ntm --help
    aliases:
      - ntm
    docs_url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm
```

## Generated Outputs
1. **Website**: Tools checklist on status page
2. **Installer**: Module installation scripts
3. **Doctor**: Check definitions with IDs
4. **Documentation**: Auto-generated tool reference

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Complete manifest covering all 40+ tools
- [ ] Parser library in packages/manifest
- [ ] Generator for website content
- [ ] Generator for installer modules
- [ ] Generator for doctor checks
- [ ] CI validates manifest syntax

## Considerations
- Keep YAML human-readable
- Use consistent IDs across the project
- Document the schema thoroughly
- Version the manifest format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:21:39.336054Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:55:29.319623Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:55:29.319623Z","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All 3 children closed. Created manifest system with acfs.manifest.yaml, TypeScript parser library, and installer generator producing 13 scripts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","manifest"]}
 {"id":"bd-fed.1","title":"Create acfs.manifest.yaml with all modules","description":"# Task: Create Complete Module Manifest

## Description
Create the canonical manifest file with all 40+ tool definitions.

## File: acfs.manifest.yaml

## Structure

```yaml
version: 1
name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
id: acfs

defaults:
  user: ubuntu
  workspace_root: /data/projects
  mode: vibe
  
categories:
  - id: base
    name: System Fundamentals
    priority: 1
  - id: shell
    name: Shell Environment
    priority: 2
  # ... etc

modules:
  # Base system
  - id: base.curl
    description: HTTP client
    category: base
    install: [sudo apt-get install -y curl]
    verify: [curl --version]
    
  # ... 40+ modules
```

## Module Categories

1. **base** - System packages (curl, git, jq, etc.)
2. **users** - User/permissions setup
3. **shell** - zsh, oh-my-zsh, plugins, theme
4. **cli** - Modern CLI tools (lsd, bat, fzf, etc.)
5. **lang** - Language runtimes (bun, uv, rust, go)
6. **tools** - Dev tools (atuin, tmux, ast-grep)
7. **db** - Databases (postgres18)
8. **cloud** - Cloud CLIs (vault, wrangler, supabase, vercel)
9. **agents** - Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini)
10. **stack** - Dicklesworthstone tools (8 tools)

## Each Module Contains
- id: Stable identifier
- description: Short description
- category: Category reference
- install: List of install commands
- verify: List of verify commands
- aliases: Optional shell aliases
- docs_url: Documentation link
- dependencies: Other module IDs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 40+ modules defined
- [ ] Valid YAML syntax
- [ ] All verify commands documented
- [ ] Dependencies specified
- [ ] Categories organized logically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:11.851933Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:11:00.620729Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:11:00.620729Z","close_reason":"acfs.manifest.yaml exists with 287 lines covering all tools","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fed.1","depends_on_id":"bd-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:11.853698Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-fed.2","title":"Build manifest parser library (packages/manifest)","description":"# Task: Build Manifest Parser Library

## Description
Create a TypeScript library to parse and work with acfs.manifest.yaml.

## Location
`packages/manifest/`

## Exports

```typescript
// Parse manifest
export function parseManifest(yamlPath: string): Manifest;

// Types
export interface Manifest {
  version: number;
  name: string;
  id: string;
  defaults: ManifestDefaults;
  categories: Category[];
  modules: Module[];
}

export interface Module {
  id: string;
  description: string;
  category: string;
  install: string[];
  verify: string[];
  aliases?: string[];
  docs_url?: string;
  dependencies?: string[];
}

// Utilities
export function getModulesByCategory(manifest: Manifest, category: string): Module[];
export function getModuleDependencies(manifest: Manifest, moduleId: string): Module[];
export function validateManifest(manifest: Manifest): ValidationResult;
```

## Features
- YAML parsing with proper error messages
- Schema validation
- Dependency cycle detection
- Type-safe access

## Dependencies
- yaml (parsing)
- zod (validation)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses valid manifest
- [ ] Rejects invalid manifest with clear errors
- [ ] Type definitions exported
- [ ] Utilities work correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:23.533183Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:26.631736Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:26.631736Z","close_reason":"Completed manifest parser library with types, schema, parser, utils, and exports. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fed.2","depends_on_id":"bd-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:26:23.534830Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -114,7 +114,9 @@ print_help() {
 # Generic sections are included; project-specific sections have placeholders
 create_agents_template() {
     local project_name="$1"
-    cat > AGENTS.md << 'AGENTS_EOF'
+    local template=""
+
+    template=$(cat << 'AGENTS_EOF'
 # AGENTS.md — PROJECT_NAME_PLACEHOLDER
 
 This file was generated by `acfs newproj` as a starting template.
@@ -435,10 +437,9 @@ ubs .                                   # Whole project
 - **Important** (production): Type narrowing, division-by-zero, resource leaks
 - **Contextual** (judgment): TODO/FIXME, console logs
 AGENTS_EOF
+)
 
-    # Replace placeholder with actual project name
-    # Use portable sed syntax that works on both GNU (Linux) and BSD (macOS)
-    sed -i.bak "s|PROJECT_NAME_PLACEHOLDER|${project_name}|g" AGENTS.md && rm -f AGENTS.md.bak
+    printf '%s\n' "${template//PROJECT_NAME_PLACEHOLDER/$project_name}" > AGENTS.md
 }
 
 # ============================================================

@@ -143,6 +143,25 @@ teardown() {
     [[ "$error" == *"not found"* ]]
 }
 
+@test "create_agents_template substitutes project name without backup file" {
+    local project_dir="$TEST_DIR/template-project"
+    mkdir -p "$project_dir"
+
+    (
+        cd "$project_dir"
+        create_agents_template "Template_Project-123"
+    )
+
+    [[ -f "$project_dir/AGENTS.md" ]]
+    [[ ! -e "$project_dir/AGENTS.md.bak" ]]
+
+    run grep -F "PROJECT_NAME_PLACEHOLDER" "$project_dir/AGENTS.md"
+    assert_failure
+
+    run grep -F "Template_Project-123/" "$project_dir/AGENTS.md"
+    assert_success
+}
+
 # ============================================================
 # Help Text Tests
